evals.prompt: Calculate earthworks volumes for a Class II paved road in Uganda (rolling terrain, laterite soils). Three cross-sections at 25m intervals provide the following data: Station 0+000: cut area 12.5 m2, fill area 3.2 m2. Station 0+025: cut area 8.3 m2, fill area 6.1 m2. Station 0+050: cut area 2.1 m2, fill area 15.4 m2. Use the average end area method. Apply a shrinkage factor of 1.20 for laterite fill. Determine the net earthworks balance and whether borrow material is needed.
evals.expected_output: Cut volumes: segment 1-2 = 260.0 m3, segment 2-3 = 130.0 m3, total cut = 390.0 m3. Fill volumes: segment 1-2 = 116.25 m3, segment 2-3 = 268.75 m3, total fill (compacted) = 385.0 m3. Shrinkage-adjusted fill = 385.0 x 1.20 = 462.0 m3. Net balance = 390.0 - 462.0 = -72.0 m3. Borrow material needed: 72.0 m3.

assertions.text: Total cut volume calculated between 380-400 m3 using average end area method
Total fill volume (before shrinkage) calculated between 375-395 m3
Shrinkage factor of 1.20 applied to fill volume, giving shrinkage-adjusted fill between 450-475 m3
Net earthworks balance correctly identified as negative (borrow needed), value between -80 and -60 m3
Identifies need for borrow material and recommends Skill #28 material suitability assessment

evals.prompt: Calculate earthworks volumes for an A-road improvement scheme in the UK. The same three cross-sections at 25m intervals are provided: Station 0+000: cut area 12.5 m2, fill area 3.2 m2. Station 0+025: cut area 8.3 m2, fill area 6.1 m2. Station 0+050: cut area 2.1 m2, fill area 15.4 m2. Use the average end area method with a shrinkage factor of 1.15 for UK mixed soils. Also note the SHW Series 600 material classification requirement and any materials management considerations.
evals.expected_output: Same volume calculations as Uganda scenario. Total cut = 390.0 m3, total fill (compacted) = 385.0 m3. Shrinkage-adjusted fill = 385.0 x 1.15 = 442.75 m3. Net balance = 390.0 - 442.75 = -52.75 m3. Borrow needed. References SHW Series 600 for material classification (Class 1 general granular or Class 6 selected cohesive). Notes CL:AIRE Materials Management Plan for on-site reuse.

assertions.text: Volume calculations correct: total cut 380-400 m3, total fill 375-395 m3
UK shrinkage factor of 1.15 applied, giving shrinkage-adjusted fill between 435-455 m3
References SHW Series 600 for earthworks material classification
Mentions CL:AIRE or Materials Management Plan for materials reuse framework
Correctly identifies borrow requirement (net balance negative)

evals.prompt: Calculate earthworks volumes for a road project with only two cross-sections available: Station 0+000: cut area 2.0 m2, fill area 18.5 m2. Station 0+100: cut area 22.0 m2, fill area 0.5 m2. The stations are 100m apart. No soil type information is available. Determine the volumes and comment on the reliability of the estimate.
evals.expected_output: Cut volume = (2.0 + 22.0)/2 x 100 = 1,200 m3. Fill volume = (18.5 + 0.5)/2 x 100 = 950 m3. With assumed shrinkage 1.20: adjusted fill = 1,140 m3. Net = 1,200 - 1,140 = +60 m3 (slight surplus). However, the large difference between areas at the two stations (2.0 vs 22.0 m2 cut, 18.5 vs 0.5 m2 fill) indicates a major transition. The 100m interval is too wide for accuracy. Prismoidal method recommended. Additional intermediate cross-sections recommended at 25m intervals.

assertions.text: Flags that the 100m cross-section spacing is too wide for reliable volume estimation
Notes the large variation between cross-section areas as a source of uncertainty
Recommends additional intermediate cross-sections to improve accuracy
Suggests prismoidal method or prismoidal correction for improved accuracy with large area differences
Uses assumed shrinkage factor (no soil data) and flags this assumption explicitly

evals.prompt: Calculate earthworks for a 15 km Kenya Class B road upgrade from gravel to paved. The route traverses rolling terrain with an average cut/fill ratio of 1.2. Typical soil is lateritic gravel with 15% shrinkage factor. Maximum embankment height is 4m. Provide representative earthworks volumes for a typical 100m section with cut area 18 m2 and fill area 15 m2 at one station and cut area 10 m2 and fill area 22 m2 at the next station (50m interval). Apply Kenya Standard Specification compaction requirements and RDM 3.3 Chapter 5 earthworks standards.
evals.expected_output: Cut volume = (18+10)/2 x 50 = 700 m3. Fill volume = (15+22)/2 x 50 = 925 m3. Shrinkage-adjusted fill = 925 x 1.15 = 1063.75 m3. Net balance = 700 - 1063.75 = -363.75 m3 (borrow needed). Kenya compaction: upper 600mm at 95% MDD (T180) with G15 min, lower fill at 93% MDD (T99) with G5 min. References RDM 3.3 Chapter 5 for slope design. Identifies laterite as common Kenya fill material. Notes regional soil considerations.

assertions.text: References RDM 3.3 Chapter 5 or Section 5.2/5.3 for Kenya earthworks requirements
Uses Kenya Standard Specification compaction standards: 95% MDD (T180) for upper 600mm and 93% MDD (T99) for lower fill
Identifies Kenya soil types (laterite, lateritic gravel) and applies appropriate shrinkage factors
Applies shrinkage factor of 1.15 to fill volume and correctly identifies borrow requirement
References Skill #28 Kenya module for material suitability assessment of borrow source
